validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile on the new test file exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_safety_rails -v passes 18/18 (0.77s, up from 15 -- 3 new cases for unit_038); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 201/201 (46.6s, up from 198)

checkpoint.summary: unit_038 complete. Batch-of-1 equivalence verification locked in. Grep over skills/development-harness/commands/invoke.md + templates/workspace-commands/invoke-development-harness.md confirms both docs gate the in-tree fast path on `len(batch) == 1 AND config.execution_mode.parallelism.enabled == false` -- batch-of-1 alone does NOT take a sequential path; when parallelism is on, a batch-of-1 still fans out through worktrees. The integration half of the acceptance (logical-state equivalence between in-tree and worktree batch-of-1) was pre-satisfied by PHASE_007 unit_033's TestBatchOfOneDispatchModeEquivalence (compares unit status + evidence count + files-on-main across the two dispatch modes; also pins via copy.deepcopy that the in-tree path leaves state.execution.fleet byte-identical). New TestInvokeDocHasNoBatchOfOneSpecialCase class (3 cases) in test_safety_rails.py promotes the grep-level contract into a regression test: (a) every in-tree gating line in both docs names the parallelism=false clause; (b) no 'batch-of-1 -> sequential' antipattern phrasing anywhere; (c) the sentence routing batch-of-1 with parallelism on to the worktree fan-out path is pinned. Test suite 198 -> 201.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_039 (PHASE_009): document scope-violation always-on policy in skills/development-harness/references/phase-contract.md. The policy statement is: scope-violation detection (git diff --name-only vs merge-base, fnmatch against declared touches_paths) runs unconditionally regardless of config.execution_mode.parallelism.require_touches_paths. Rationale: touches_paths is a trust-boundary declaration, not a feature toggle -- even when it's not required as input, once it IS declared on a unit the orchestrator must enforce it because the sub-agent is untrusted. require_touches_paths only controls whether compute_parallel_batch rejects units that lack a touches_paths declaration; it does NOT relax merge-time enforcement when touches_paths IS present. Validation: grep of phase-contract.md for the policy statement + unit test that the policy text is present.
